Python is a high-level programming language widely used
for web development, AI, automation, and backend systems.
It is easy to learn and has many frameworks like FastAPI and Django.

Backend development refers to server-side development.
It handles databases, APIs, authentication, business logic,
and communication between frontend and database systems.

Popular backend frameworks include FastAPI, Django, Express.js, and Spring Boot.

FastAPI is a modern Python backend framework used for building APIs.
It is extremely fast and supports async programming.
FastAPI is commonly used in AI applications and microservices.

Naeem Sajjad is a 24-year-old software developer from Hasilpur, Pakistan.
He is a MERN stack and full stack developer who works on modern web applications,
APIs, AI integrations, and chatbot systems. Naeem is passionate about learning
AI engineering, prompt engineering, vector databases, FAISS, RAG systems,
and backend development technologies.

Naeem Sajjad studied at COMSATS University Islamabad, Vehari Campus.
He completed his degree with a CGPA of 3.62. During his academic journey,
he developed strong programming and software engineering skills and gained
experience in web development, backend systems, databases, and frontend technologies.

Naeem Sajjad currently works at Devline Solutions as a Full Stack Developer.
He builds modern applications using technologies like React, FastAPI,
Node.js, Express.js, MongoDB, and AI APIs. He is interested in building
production-level AI systems, chatbots, semantic search engines, and RAG applications.

Naeem Sajjad lives in Hasilpur with his family. His father’s name is Sajjad Hussain.
Naeem also has a brother named Waseem Sajjad, who owns a perfume business.
Naeem enjoys technology, software development, AI learning, and building innovative products.

AI Tech Solutions – Employee Handbook & 
Knowledge Base 
1. Company Overview 
AI Tech Solutions is a software company specializing in artificial intelligence applications, 
cloud platforms, and ente

a software company specializing in artificial intelligence applications, 
cloud platforms, and enterprise automation systems. The company was founded in 2021 with 
the mission of helping businesses automate repetitive tasks using modern AI technologies. 
The company provides services in: 
• AI chatbot development 
• Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems 
• Cloud deployment 
• MERN stack applications 
• Data analytics dashboards 
• API integrations 
The headquarters is located in Lahore,

plications 
• Data analytics dashboards 
• API integrations 
The headquarters is located in Lahore, Pakistan, with remote employees working from 
different countries. 
 
2. Company Vision 
The vision of AI Tech Solutions is to create intelligent systems that improve productivity and 
reduce manual effort for businesses worldwide. 
The company believes that artificial intelligence should: 
• Assist humans instead of replacing them 
• Increase efficiency 
• Improve decision-making • Make informati

humans instead of replacing them 
• Increase efficiency 
• Improve decision-making • Make information more accessible 
 
3. Employee Working Hours 
The standard working schedule is: 
• Monday to Friday 
• 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM 
• One-hour lunch break 
Employees working remotely must: 
• Attend daily standup meetings 
• Update Jira tasks regularly 
• Respond to Slack messages during working hours 
Flexible timing is allowed for senior developers and project managers. 
 
4. Leave Policy 
Employees r

exible timing is allowed for senior developers and project managers. 
 
4. Leave Policy 
Employees receive: 
• 18 annual paid leaves 
• 10 sick leaves 
• 5 casual leaves 
Unused annual leaves can be carried forward to the next year up to a maximum of 10 days. 
Emergency leave requests must be submitted through the HR portal. 
Maternity leave policy: 
• 90 days paid leave 
Paternity leave policy: 
• 14 days paid leave

5. Engineering Department 
The engineering department is divided into several teams: Frontend Team

• React applications 
• Next.js projects 
• UI/UX implementation

Backend Team

6. RAG System Ar

• Embedding pipelines 
• Vector databases 
• Prompt engineering 
• RAG systems 
 
6. RAG System Architecture 
The company uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems for internal document search. 
The RAG pipeline includes: 
1. Document ingestion 
2. Text extraction 
3. Chunking 
4. Embedding generation 
5. Vector storage 
6. Similarity retrieval 
7. LLM response generation 
The vector database currently used is FAISS for local development and Pinecone for cloud 
production systems.  
7. Chunk

currently used is FAISS for local development and Pinecone for cloud 
production systems.  
7. Chunking Strategy 
The AI team uses overlapping chunking for document processing. 
Default configuration: 
• Chunk size: 500 characters 
• Overlap: 100 characters 
The purpose of overlap is to preserve context between chunks. 
Example: 
If one chunk ends with a sentence and another chunk starts immediately after it, overlap 
ensures the sentence meaning is not lost. 
 
8. Embedding Models 
The company

ly after it, overlap 
ensures the sentence meaning is not lost. 
 
8. Embedding Models 
The company currently uses multiple embedding providers. 
OpenAI Embeddings 
Used for: 
• semantic search 
• chatbot memory retrieval 
• document similarity 
Gemini Embeddings 
Used for: 
• Google ecosystem integration 
• lightweight semantic indexing 
Sentence Transformers 
Used for: 
• open-source projects 
• offline embedding generation 
Embedding vectors are stored in vector databases for retrieval operat

offline embedding generation 
Embedding vectors are stored in vector databases for retrieval operations.  
9. Vector Databases 
The engineering department works with several vector databases. 
FAISS 
A local vector similarity library created by Meta. 
Advantages: 
• very fast 
• lightweight 
• good for local projects 
Disadvantages: 
• not ideal for distributed cloud scaling 
Pinecone 
A managed cloud vector database. 
Advantages: 
• scalable 
• production-ready 
• metadata filtering 
Disadvant

cloud vector database. 
Advantages: 
• scalable 
• production-ready 
• metadata filtering 
Disadvantages: 
• paid service 
 
10. Authentication System 
The backend authentication system uses JWT tokens. 
Authentication flow: 
1. User logs in 
2. Server validates credentials 
3. JWT token generated 
4. Frontend stores token 
5. Protected routes verify token Refresh tokens are used for long sessions. 
Passwords are hashed using bcrypt before storage. 
 
11. API Development Standards 
All APIs must

. 
Passwords are hashed using bcrypt before storage. 
 
11. API Development Standards 
All APIs must: 
• use REST conventions 
• return JSON responses 
• include proper status codes 
• handle exceptions gracefully 
Example status codes: 
• 200 OK 
• 201 Created 
• 400 Bad Request 
• 401 Unauthorized 
• 404 Not Found 
• 500 Internal Server Error 
 
12. Database Standards 
The company uses: 
• PostgreSQL 
• MongoDB 
• Redis 
MongoDB is preferred for flexible document storage. 
Redis is mainly used

reSQL 
• MongoDB 
• Redis 
MongoDB is preferred for flexible document storage. 
Redis is mainly used for: 
• caching 
• session management 
• rate limiting 
 
13. Deployment Process 
Applications are deployed using Docker containers. Deployment flow: 
1. Push code to GitHub 
2. GitHub Actions run tests 
3. Docker image is built 
4. Image deployed to cloud server 
Production servers are hosted on AWS. 
 
14. Security Policies 
Employees must: 
• use strong passwords 
• enable two-factor authentic

WS. 
 
14. Security Policies 
Employees must: 
• use strong passwords 
• enable two-factor authentication 
• avoid sharing credentials 
• rotate API keys regularly 
Sensitive data should never be exposed in logs. 
 
15. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q1: What is RAG? 
RAG stands for Retrieval-Augmented Generation. It combines information retrieval with 
language generation. 
Q2: What is an embedding? 
An embedding is a numerical representation of text that captures semantic meaning. 
Q3: Why use ch

An embedding is a numerical representation of text that captures semantic meaning. 
Q3: Why use chunk overlap? 
Overlap prevents important contextual information from being lost between chunks. 
Q4: What is FAISS? 
FAISS is a vector similarity search library used for fast semantic retrieval. 
Q5: What is semantic search? Semantic search finds information based on meaning rather than exact keywords. 
 
16. AI Chatbot Guidelines 
The chatbot should: 
• avoid hallucinations 
• answer only from pr

ds. 
 
16. AI Chatbot Guidelines 
The chatbot should: 
• avoid hallucinations 
• answer only from provided context 
• provide concise responses 
• mention uncertainty when information is missing 
The chatbot must prioritize retrieved context over assumptions. 
 
17. Future AI Roadmap 
The company plans to: 
• build multimodal RAG systems 
• support image embeddings 
• integrate voice assistants 
• add long-term memory systems 
• improve retrieval accuracy using reranking

All tools are connected through internal APIs.
